other_observers: Many Bonny CBC participants. I observed the bird with Joey Kellner, Andrew Spencer, and Cole Wild.; Mark Peterson first found the bird on 12/22/05, soon joined by Glenn Walbek, Tony Leukering, Scott Shaum, and Loch Kilpatrick.
specific_location: S. Side of Bonny Reservoir in brushy ditch-like area just east or south of dirt berm that divides the SWA and the State Park portions of the dirt road.
description: A rufous-sided towhee lacking nearly all white on the upperparts. Head, nape, and upper back appeared a uniform chocolate brown color. The lower back appeared a slightly grayer shade of brown. On the upper breast, the dark hood came to a point on the white lower breast. The belly was also white. The sides were brick red. The wing coverts and flight feathers were dark brown with slightly lighter brown highlights. A white spot was formed at the base of the primaries, with white shafts running down the outer two or so primaries from the spot. The tertials appeared to have long white panels. The tail also appeared dark brown, with white corners on the outer tail feathers. The legs and feet were pink. The bill was dark gray, sparrow-like in structure. The iris appeared brown with just a tint of reddish warmth.Behaviors: The bird was skulking in heavy brush and junipers. # of Birds:Call: Song not heard. Bird gave the distinctive EATO zhwink call several times, as well as thinner, more generic sounding chip call notes. The call note was verified in the field with an iPod and external speakers.Plumage: possible HY female per Chris Wood
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
